title: [InstCombine] Add a >= b - a<=b to scmp and ucmp folds
summary:
The developers generalized the pattern by recognizing that `sext(icmp) + zext(icmp)` can be simplified to `scmp/ucmp` when the comparisons are complementary—either both strict (`<`/`>`) or both non-strict (`≤`/`≥`). They extended the transform to handle both signed and unsigned comparisons uniformly.

They also added support for constant canonicalization: `sext(x > C) + zext(x < C+2)` or `sext(x < C+2) + zext(x > C)` simplifies to `scmp/ucmp(x, C+1)`. This captures cases where the original comparisons are offset by 2, representing a midpoint condition.

The patch introduces two key improvements:
1. **Predicate pairing**: Expanded from only `LT`/`GT` to also include `LE`/`GE`.
2. **Constant folding**: Added logic to detect and fold constant offsets into a single comparison against the midpoint.

This makes the optimization more robust, covering both variable and constant comparisons while maintaining the same core transformation principle.
